# Title: ASIN creation policy

Title: ASIN creation policy

URL Source: https://sellercentral.amazon.com/help/hub/reference/external/G201844590

Markdown Content:
This article applies to selling in: **United States**

On this page

[ASIN creation by sellers without a selling role assigned by the brand owner in Brand Registry](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/help/hub/reference/external/G201844590#mnd_2jc_jcb)

[ASIN creation limit (8575 error)](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/help/hub/reference/external/G201844590#mnd_2jc_jcb-1)

[Variation relationship policy](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/help/hub/reference/external/G201844590#mnd_2jc_jcb-3)

To sell a product that is already available on Amazon, you can add an offer to the existing ASIN. If your product is not in the Amazon catalog, you will have to create a new ASIN. You can do either in our [Add Products](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/productsearch) tool.

Newly created ASINs must adhere to the following requirements to maintain product integrity:

Newly created ASINs must adhere to the following requirements to maintain product integrity:

*   The ASIN created must be an active product from the brand owner or manufacturer. It cannot be a discontinued product that is past its useful life, inclusive of expiration and functional factors the define the product’s life.
*   The ASIN created must be a product configuration created by the brand owner or manufacturer.
*   Sellers should not list non-standard multipacks that are not created by the brand owner or manufacturer.
*   Sellers must follow the additional guidelines in the [Product Bundling policy](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/gp/help/external/G200442350) to create bundles.
*   ASIN creation must comply with [Intellectual Property Policy for sellers](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/gp/help/external/G201361070).

## ASIN creation by sellers without a selling role assigned by the brand owner in Brand Registry

## ASIN creation by sellers without a selling role assigned by the brand owner in Brand Registry

To protect the customer shopping experience, we limit sellers who do not have a selling role assigned by a brand from creating new ASINs under that brand when the brand is enrolled in Brand Registry.

## ASIN creation limit (8575 error)

To protect the onsite shopping experience for our customers, we limit the number of listings (new ASINs) that you can create in a given week until you establish a sales history with Amazon. As you increase your sales, your capacity will increase. We encourage you to prioritize the products you are listing to increase your sales quickly.

If you have created a high number of new listings, we retain the right to temporarily suspend your ability to create new listings. This temporary suspension will be automatically lifted at 00:00 GMT on the first day of each week (Sunday), enabling you to resume creating new listings within the designated capacity.

To troubleshoot the error, you can:

*   divide your templates for more than one submission in other to meet your listing capacity (that is, send a limit of product per week).
*   contact the Selling Partner Support to increase the ASIN creation limit.

If you think your listing creation privileges have been removed in error, contact [Selling Partner Support](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/cu/contact-us) or use Amazon's Seller Assistant.

## Listing creation limit

To ensure the Amazon product catalog remains focused on discoverability and customer demand, we may have to limit your ability to create new listings if your existing catalog contains a large number of listings that are not generating sales. This is done through the listing creation limit, which restricts the number of new listings you can add to your catalog.

If the number of listings in your catalog exceeds our threshold, your ability to create new listings will be restricted until you reduce your catalog size.

We evaluate your catalog daily against the listing creation limit policies. This may result in updates to the listing creation threshold or reinstatement of your creation ability, depending on your product listing deletions or an improvement in sales performance. For more information, go to "Delete product and listing" section in [Stop selling a product](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/gp/help/external/G200216110).

You can expect to receive relevant communications about the listing creation limit through emails and Cards on Seller Central Homepage. This will include warnings about approaching listing creation restrictions, notifications of actual enforcement of the listing creation limits, and communications about the reinstatement of your listing creation ability.

## Variation relationship policy

## Variation relationship policy

Variations are sets of products that are related to one another. Good variation relationship listings allow buyers to compare and choose products based on different attributes such as size, color, or other characteristics from the available options on a single product detail page. For more information, go to [Variation Relationship Overview](https://sellercentral.amazon.com/gp/help/external/8831).

The following prohibited practices are misuses of standalone ASINs and/or variations, either of the parent ASIN or in parent-child relationships. These practices create a negative customer experience and can result in your ASIN creation or selling privileges being temporarily or permanently removed:

*   Changing the product’s detail page (parent or child) to become fundamentally different from the original product listed
*   Changing the parent product's detail page so it does not match the children
*   Adding incorrect child variations that are not true variations of the parent product. This includes but is not limited to:
    *   adding products that are fundamentally different from the parent ASIN
    *   adding products images or names that are that are fundamentally different from the parent ASIN
    *   adding products that are newer versions or models of the parent ASIN

*   Adding multi-pack variations that aren’t manufacturer-created to an already existing parent
